headlines.text: Trump says Iran peace deal 'proceeding nicely' but no signing imminent as blockade holds
Gunman killed after opening fire at White House checkpoint; bystander wounded
Tulsi Gabbard resigns as DNI to care for husband battling rare bone cancer
Mandalorian & Grogu hauls in $100M domestic over Memorial Day — Disney's lowest Star Wars opening ever
US markets closed for Memorial Day; stock futures rise on Iran deal optimism
Gas prices hit four-year high at $4.51 national average over holiday weekend
WTI crude drops ~5% toward $91 as Iran deal hopes cool oil rally
Iranian delegation in Qatar to hash out Hormuz and uranium disputes with US negotiators
Stephen Colbert's Late Show airs final episode this week amid media merger chaos
European and Asian markets rally Monday on Iran peace talk momentum

breaking.text: Iran-US peace framework talks intensify: high-level Iranian delegation now in Qatar; WTI oil drops 5% on deal hopes

stories.hed: Trump Says Iran Deal 'Proceeding Nicely,' Iran Says Not So Fast
stories.dek: The US and Iran are swapping proposals through Pakistani mediators like they're playing nuclear telephone, with sticking points including who controls the Strait of Hormuz, Iran's enriched uranium, and who blinks first. Trump is demanding Abraham Accords sign-ups as a bonus prize; Iran's foreign ministry is calling the agreement 'not imminent,' which is diplomatic for 'we haven't agreed to anything yet.'

stories.hed: Oil Slides 5% as Hormuz Deal Hopes Grow — But Brent Already Below $100
stories.dek: Global oil markets are betting the Strait of Hormuz might actually reopen, sending WTI crude tumbling toward $91 a barrel on Monday. European stocks surged 1.75% and Asian markets followed suit, while traders cautiously price in a deal that neither side has fully agreed to.

stories.hed: Gunman Killed at White House Checkpoint; Third Shooting Near Trump in a Month
stories.dek: 21-year-old Nasire Best opened fire on a Secret Service booth at 17th and Pennsylvania Avenue Saturday evening before being fatally shot by officers; a bystander was also wounded. Court records show Best had twice previously tried to enter the White House and was ordered to stay away — someone really should have updated his contact list.

stories.hed: Tulsi Gabbard Out as DNI; Fourth Cabinet Woman to Exit Trump's Second Term
stories.dek: Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard resigned Friday, citing her husband Abraham's diagnosis with an extremely rare form of bone cancer, with her last day set for June 30. She is the fourth woman to leave Trump's cabinet this year, following Pam Bondi, Kristi Noem, and Lori Chavez-DeRemer — a pattern the White House has chosen not to comment on.

stories.hed: An Iran Deal That Leaves the Nukes Unresolved May Be Almost as Divisive as the War Itself
stories.dek: CNN's analysis points out that Trump faces heat from hawks on the right (Lindsey Graham, Ted Cruz) for going too soft, and from Democrats for starting an unpopular war without Congressional approval in the first place. The proposed framework would reopen Hormuz and pause fighting but leave Iran's nuclear program 'largely unresolved' — which is exactly the kind of outcome that satisfies nobody and gets declared a win anyway.

markets.context: US equity markets are closed for Memorial Day; Friday's last close showed stocks rising on Iran deal optimism while oil futures tumbled ~5% Monday on hopes the Strait of Hormuz may soon reopen.

gas.note: Enjoy that Memorial Day road trip — your tank costs roughly $1.38 more per gallon than it did this time last year, courtesy of a war neither Congress nor your summer budget approved.

popCulture.hed: Mandalorian & Grogu Opens to $100M — Baby Yoda's Back, Critics Unmoved
popCulture.dek: The first Star Wars film in seven years pulled in $100M domestic and $163M globally over Memorial Day weekend, earning an A- CinemaScore from fans who clearly missed Baby Yoda more than critics did (62% RT vs 89% audience). It's Disney's lowest Star Wars opening in the modern era, but with a lean $165M budget and a galaxy of merchandise, the studio is choosing to call it a win.

genZ.definition: A viral TikTok audio format resurrected by The Devil Wears Prada 2 hitting theaters May 1, where creators film two contrasting shots — one polished and correct, one chaotic and wrong — delivering a withering Miranda Priestly-style judgment. It has taken over FYPs in May 2026 as the defining vibe-contrast format of the summer, used for everything from makeup routines to roasting coworkers.
genZ.usage: POV: Trump negotiating the Iran deal [polished shot: Rubio in a suit] vs. Trump posting 'DEAL OR MILITARY ACTION' on Truth Social at 11pm [chaotic shot] ...and Emily, that's all.

tldr: America celebrated Memorial Day with a shooting outside the White House, $4.51 gas, and Baby Yoda's underwhelming big-screen comeback — while diplomats in Qatar quietly tried to end a war that tanked all three. The Iran peace deal is close enough to crash oil prices but not close enough to actually exist yet, which pretty much sums up the whole spring.
